title: arum dioscoridis var. viridulum
intro: Arum dioscoridis var. viridulum, also known as the wild asparagus or green asparagus, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the asparagus family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating urinary tract issues, such as kidney stones and gravel, due to its diuretic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Arum dioscoridis var. viridulum include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and acting as a natural diuretic, which can help in relieving symptoms of urinary tract infections and other related conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, or dried and used as a powder, and it can also be used in conjunction with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
